skill.name: firecrawl-parse
skill.description: Convert supported local documents into clean Markdown files with Firecrawl. The skill can also request summaries or answers about the parsed document.
skill.summary: Parse local documents into Markdown files using Firecrawl.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick, weak crypto, and path traversal findings are false positives from Markdown text and a documentation link. The real risk is medium because the skill authorizes Firecrawl CLI commands that process local files and may send document contents to an external service.

medium_findings.title: Local Document Contents May Be Sent to Firecrawl
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to parse local files with the Firecrawl CLI and mentions upload size and credit usage. This appears legitimate, but sensitive local documents may leave the workspace for external processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and upload or credit notes strongly indicate Firecrawl service processing. I found no evidence of malicious exfiltration, but local file disclosure is a real privacy risk.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Are Authorized
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash execution of firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands. This is expected for the skill, but it requires trust in the Firecrawl CLI and any package invoked through npx.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block explicitly grants command execution for Firecrawl commands. The examples use fixed command forms, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of command injection.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Writes Are Directed to Local Output Files
low_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs use of an output path under .firecrawl. This is normal for document conversion, but users should review output paths before writing files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output behavior is clearly documented and scoped to generated parse files. I did not find evidence that the skill overwrites sensitive paths by itself.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Pattern Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts point to Markdown inline code, documentation examples, or frontmatter text. No Ruby execution or cryptographic operation appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose, option tables, or inline command references. There is no source code implementing Ruby backticks or weak hashing.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is a Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The ../ sequence is used in a Markdown link to a related skill file. It is not used as runtime file input or filesystem traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 61 is only a relative Markdown link under a See also section. I found no code path that consumes this link for filesystem access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Access to Firecrawl Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools block permits Firecrawl CLI execution from Bash, including npx firecrawl. Marketplace users should understand that this skill invokes external tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command permission is explicit in the skill metadata. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a meaningful execution capability.

content.value_statement: Local documents are hard to review when their text stays locked in PDFs, spreadsheets, or word processor files. This skill uses Firecrawl to create Markdown, summaries, and document answers for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Converts supported local documents into Markdown files on disk.
Supports PDF, DOCX, DOC, ODT, RTF, XLSX, XLS, HTML, HTM, and XHTML inputs.
Saves parsed output to a chosen file path, usually under .firecrawl.
Requests an AI-generated summary of a parsed document.
Asks a question about a document and saves the answer.
limitations: Requires the Firecrawl CLI or npx Firecrawl command to be available.
May upload local document contents to Firecrawl for processing.
The documented maximum upload size is 50 MB per file.
Large parsed files should be read incrementally instead of loaded all at once.

use_cases.title: Review a Research PDF
use_cases.description: Convert a paper into Markdown, then ask for a short summary or main conclusions.

use_cases.title: Extract Contract Text
use_cases.description: Turn a local DOCX or PDF contract into Markdown for review and comparison.

use_cases.title: Prepare Documents for Analysis
use_cases.description: Batch parse office files into Markdown before searching, summarizing, or indexing them.

prompt_templates.title: Parse One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion of a local document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse this local file into Markdown and save the result under .firecrawl: ./document.pdf

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a concise summary after parsing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert ./report.docx into Markdown, then save a summary in .firecrawl/report-summary.md.

prompt_templates.title: Ask a Document Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Use Firecrawl query mode for targeted extraction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse ./proposal.pdf and answer this question: What are the pricing terms and key deadlines?

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Batch Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for several local files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .firecrawl output plan for these local files, avoid re-parsing existing outputs, and note credit-sensitive files.

output_examples.input: A user asks to parse a PDF named annual-report.pdf.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a Markdown file under .firecrawl and reports the saved path with a brief note about file size.

output_examples.input: A user asks for the main conclusions from a research paper.
output_examples.output: The assistant saves a summary file and provides a concise conclusion list based on the parsed document.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a contract includes renewal terms.
output_examples.output: The assistant saves a question-answer result and highlights the relevant renewal terms found in the document.

best_practices: Confirm that the document can be shared with Firecrawl before parsing sensitive files.
Save outputs under .firecrawl and add that directory to .gitignore.
Read large parsed files in small sections with search tools.
anti_patterns: Do not parse confidential documents without user approval.
Do not stream large parsed documents directly into the chat context.
Do not use this skill for URLs; use a scraping workflow instead.

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It documents support for PDF, DOCX, DOC, ODT, RTF, XLSX, XLS, HTML, HTM, and XHTML files.

faq.question: Where does the parsed output go?
faq.answer: The skill recommends saving output files under .firecrawl with the Firecrawl output option.

faq.question: Can it summarize a document?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a summary option that saves an AI-generated summary.

faq.question: Can it answer questions about a document?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a query option for asking a question about parsed content.

faq.question: Does it work for web pages?
faq.answer: This skill is for local files. The skill says to use a scraping workflow for URLs.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: Local file contents may be processed by Firecrawl, so users should avoid sensitive files unless sharing is approved.
